mnemonics: DOTTING THE CLASS: Class A (1-126), B (128-191), C (192-223), D (224-239), E (240-255) - Remember the starting numbers 1, 128, 192, 224, 240
keyPoints: IPv4 addresses are 32-bit numbers represented in dotted-decimal notation (e.g., 192.168.1.1)
The address is divided into network and host portions using a subnet mask
commonMistakes: Confusing public IP addresses with private IP addresses (e.g., using 192.168.x.x on the public internet)
Incorrectly calculating subnet masks and network boundaries
Forgetting that the first address in a subnet is the network address and the last is the broadcast address
